title: MVP Infrastructure as Code (Bicep)
description:
## Overview

Complete Bicep IaC to provision the entire MVP from scratch. One `az deployment group create` on a fresh resource group stands up everything for upload → transcribe → score → display. No manual Azure Portal clicks required.

## Prerequisites

- Azure CLI installed and logged in (`az login`)
- Subscription with Owner role
- Resource providers registered (deploy.sh handles this)

## What Bicep Creates (Everything)

### 1. AI Speech Service
- Name: psr-speech-dev, Kind: SpeechServices
- SKU: F0 (free tier — 5 hrs/mo transcription, covers ~7 sermons)
- Location: eastus2
- Fast transcription API proven in POC #6 (3,762 words in 44s)

### 2. Azure OpenAI Account
- Name: psr-openai-dev, Kind: OpenAI
- SKU: S0 (standard)
- Location: eastus2
- Custom subdomain: psr-openai-dev (required for SDK auth)
- Public network access: Enabled

### 3. Azure OpenAI Model Deployments
- `o4-mini`: model o4-mini, version 2025-04-16, Standard, 80K TPM — Pass 1: Biblical Analysis
- `gpt-41`: model gpt-4.1, version 2025-04-14, Standard, 50K TPM — Pass 2: Structure & Content
- `gpt-41-mini`: model gpt-4.1-mini, version 2025-04-14, Standard, 80K TPM — Pass 3: Delivery + Classification

Note: Model versions may need updating if deploying later. Check `az cognitiveservices model list --location eastus2 --query "[?model.name=='o4-mini']"` for current versions.

### 4. Storage Account (Blob)
- Name: psrstoragedev (globally unique)
- SKU: Standard_LRS, Kind: StorageV2
- Container: sermon-audio (private)
- Purpose: uploaded audio files

### 5. Cosmos DB (Serverless)
- Account: psr-cosmos-dev, API: NoSQL
- Capacity: Serverless ($0 at idle)
- Database: psr
- Container: sermons (partition key: /id) — metadata, transcript, PSR scores, status

### 6. Key Vault
- Name: psr-kv-dev, SKU: Standard
- Access: RBAC (no access policies)
- Secrets auto-populated by Bicep/deploy.sh: speech-key, openai-key, openai-endpoint, speech-endpoint, cosmos-connection-string, storage-connection-string

### 7. Log Analytics Workspace
- Name: psr-logs-dev, SKU: PerGB2018, Retention: 30 days
- Required dependency for App Insights

### 8. Application Insights
- Name: psr-insights-dev, Kind: web
- Linked to Log Analytics workspace

### 9. Function App (Python, Consumption)
- Name: psr-functions-dev
- Runtime: Python 3.12, OS: Linux, Plan: Consumption (Y1)
- App Settings via Key Vault references: SPEECH_KEY, SPEECH_ENDPOINT, OPENAI_KEY, OPENAI_ENDPOINT, COSMOS_CONNECTION_STRING, STORAGE_CONNECTION_STRING
- AzureWebJobsStorage: direct connection string (runtime needs it at startup before Key Vault is reachable)
- Extensions: Durable Functions (azure-functions-durable)
- Managed Identity: System-assigned
- Python deps (requirements.txt): azure-functions, azure-functions-durable, openai, parselmouth, azure-cognitiveservices-speech, azure-cosmos, azure-storage-blob

### 10. Static Web App
- Name: psr-web-dev, SKU: Free
- Build: Next.js. Bicep creates resource; GitHub Actions deploys code.

### 11. RBAC Assignments (Function App managed identity)
- Key Vault Secrets User on psr-kv-dev
- Storage Blob Data Contributor on storage account
- Cognitive Services User on psr-openai-dev + psr-speech-dev
- Cosmos DB Built-in Data Contributor on psr-cosmos-dev

## Bicep File Structure

```
infra/
  main.bicep           — orchestrator, calls all modules
  main.bicepparam      — dev environment parameter values
  modules/
    speech.bicep       — AI Speech service (F0)
    openai.bicep       — OpenAI account + 3 model deployments
    storage.bicep      — storage account + blob container
    cosmos.bicep       — Cosmos DB serverless + database + container
    keyvault.bicep     — Key Vault + secrets
    monitoring.bicep   — Log Analytics + App Insights
    functions.bicep    — Function App + consumption plan + settings
    staticwebapp.bicep — Static Web App
    rbac.bicep         — all managed identity role assignments
  deploy.sh            — registers providers, creates RG, deploys Bicep
```

## deploy.sh

1. Register required resource providers (idempotent — safe to re-run)
2. Create resource group if not exists
3. `az deployment group create` with Bicep
4. Populate Key Vault secrets from deployed resource keys
5. Print summary of created resources and endpoints

## Post-Deploy Steps

1. Deploy Function App code: `func azure functionapp publish psr-functions-dev`
2. Link Static Web App to GitHub repo (or deploy via SWA CLI)

## Cost at Idle: ~$0.51/mo

Storage ~$0.50, everything else $0 (serverless/free tiers). Per-sermon: ~$0.75.

## Parameters (main.bicepparam)

- `environment`: dev (affects naming: psr-*-dev)
- `location`: eastus2
- `openaiModelVersions`: object with model versions (updatable without changing modules)

title: POC #10: Redefine Time in the Word as biblical content density
close_reason: Redefined Time in the Word from direct quotation % to biblical content density. Spurgeon Compel Them: 85.9→92.7, Immutability: 72.8→87.5. Piper: 83.9→87.7. Scheer correctly drops 84.7→78.7 (30% anecdotes). Updated prompts in all 3 POC files + sermonplan + research docs.

title: Request Azure OpenAI quota for GPT-5-mini, GPT-5-nano, GPT-4.1-nano
description:
## Quota Request

Submit form at https://aka.ms/oai/stuquotarequest

- Subscription: 80b31d19-b663-4936-b8ee-93f7af5b1d27
- Resource: psr-openai-dev
- Region: East US 2

### Models to Request

| Model | Deployment Type | Requested TPM |
|---|---|---|
| gpt-5-mini | GlobalStandard | 50K |
| gpt-5-nano | GlobalStandard | 50K |
| gpt-4.1-nano | GlobalStandard | 50K |

### Current Deployments (working, no changes needed)
- o4-mini (Pass 1: Biblical Analysis) — KEEP, best reasoning model available
- gpt-41 / GPT-4.1 (Pass 2: Structure & Content)
- gpt-41-mini / GPT-4.1-mini (Pass 3: Delivery + Classification)

### Upgrade Path Once Quota Approved
- gpt-5-mini replaces GPT-4.1 for Pass 2 — better benchmarks, 75% cheaper ($0.25/$2.00 vs $2.00/$8.00)
- gpt-5-nano replaces GPT-4.1-mini for Pass 3 + classification — 85% cheaper ($0.05/$0.40 vs $0.40/$1.60)
- gpt-4.1-nano is fallback for classification if GPT-5 quota takes a while

### Cost Impact
Current: ~$0.086/sermon → After upgrade: ~$0.040/sermon (-53%)

### Notes
- GPT-5 quota requests may take days to weeks per Microsoft forums
- o4-mini stays for Pass 1 — purpose-built for reasoning, proven in POCs
- Nothing is blocked; current models are solid

title: API Layer (Azure Functions HTTP triggers)
description:
Three HTTP endpoints the frontend needs, running as Azure Functions:

## POST /api/sermons — Upload

Accept audio upload, validate, kick off processing.

Validation:
- File type: MP3, WAV, M4A only (check Content-Type + magic bytes)
- File size: max 100MB (Azure Functions consumption plan request limit)
- WAV files over 100MB: reject with error suggesting MP3 conversion. Fast transcription API accepts MP3 directly — no need to convert to WAV.
- Duration check: not possible pre-upload without processing. WPM sanity check catches bad files post-transcription.

**Note:** Frontend performs client-side file size and type validation before upload (check file.size and file.type in JS). This prevents users from hitting the Functions 100MB request limit with a confusing server-side error. Backend still validates as a safety net.

Flow:
1. Validate file
2. Create sermon record in Cosmos DB (status: processing, title: user-provided or filename, pastor: user-provided or null, uploadedAt: now)
3. Store audio in Blob Storage (sermon-audio container, key: {id}/{original_filename})
4. Start Durable Functions orchestrator (pass sermon ID + blob URL + user-provided metadata)
5. Return sermon ID immediately

Response: {id, status: processing}

## GET /api/sermons — Feed List

Return all sermons for the feed page. No pagination for MVP.

Response: [{id, title, pastor, date, duration, status, sermonType, compositePsr}]
- status values: processing, complete, failed
- compositePsr is null when status is processing or failed
- title/pastor come from user input (if provided) or LLM metadata extraction (step 4 of pipeline)
- If still processing and no user-provided title, defaults to original filename

## GET /api/sermons/{id} — Full Detail

Return full sermon document from Cosmos DB. Frontend reads: metadata, all 8 category scores + weights + reasoning, composite, strengths/improvements, transcript with segment classifications, radar chart data.

When status is failed:
- error: human-readable error message
- failedAt: ISO 8601 timestamp
- compositePsr, categories, transcript, strengths, improvements: all null

## CORS

Configure for Static Web App origin. In dev: localhost:3000.

## Data Contracts

Defined in docs/frontend-spec.md. The API returns Cosmos DB documents directly — no transformation layer for MVP.

title: Sermon Processing Pipeline (Durable Functions)
description:
Durable Functions orchestrator that takes uploaded audio through the full pipeline:

1. Transcribe via Azure AI Speech fast transcription API
2. Audio analysis via Parselmouth (pitch, volume, pauses)
   (Steps 1 and 2 run in parallel — neither depends on the other)

3. Fan-out 5 parallel passes (all need transcript; Pass 3 also needs Parselmouth data):
   - Pass 1: Biblical Analysis (o4-mini) — Biblical Accuracy, Time in the Word, Passage Focus
   - Pass 2: Structure & Content (GPT-4.1) — Clarity, Application, Engagement
   - Pass 3: Delivery (GPT-4.1-mini) — Delivery, Emotional Range
   - Sermon type classification + metadata extraction (GPT-4.1-mini) — expository/topical/survey, title, pastor name, main passage
   - Segment classification (GPT-4.1-mini) — label transcript segments as scripture/teaching/application/anecdote/illustration/prayer/transition (required for frontend transcript viewer color-coding)

4. Fan-in

5. Score normalization by sermon type (pure code, no LLM) — needs sermon type from classification

6. Store results in Cosmos DB, update status to complete

## Parselmouth Hosting

Parselmouth is a single 36MB .so with standard libc deps (no exotic native binaries). It CAN run on Azure Functions Linux consumption plan — the sandbox supports standard C++ libs. The 36MB adds to cold start but is within the 1.5GB memory limit. For MVP, deploy Parselmouth as part of the Function App package. If cold starts become a problem, move to a dedicated activity function on Premium plan later.

## Parselmouth Noise Floor Filtering (POC #8)

POC #8 (Scheer sermon) reported 384 dB intensity range — physically impossible. Caused by near-silence frames in the MP3. Fix: filter out frames below a noise floor (bottom 5th percentile of intensity values) before computing intensity stats. Pitch already filters unvoiced frames (pv > 0); apply the same pattern to intensity. Expected real range: 30-80 dB.

## Sermon Type Classification Fix (POC #8)

POC #8 misclassified Scheer's verse-by-verse 1 Peter 1:1-2 sermon as topical (85%) because the classifier only saw the first 2000-3000 chars — which was all intro anecdote. This inflated the composite by ~3 points via normalization bumps.

Fix: send more transcript context to the classifier — first 1500 + middle 1500 + last 1500 chars instead of just first 2000-3000. Alternatively, add a post-classification sanity check: if Pass 1 detects a clear main passage with >40% focus AND scripture density >0.5 refs/min, override topical→expository.

## Error/Retry Strategy

Durable Functions built-in retry policies:
- Transcription (fast API): retry 2x, 30s backoff. If permanent fail → status: failed, error: transcription_error
- Parselmouth audio analysis: retry 1x. If fail → proceed WITHOUT audio metrics, flag delivery/emotional_range scores as partial (audio_metrics: null). Graceful degradation.
- Each LLM pass (1-3): retry 3x, exponential backoff starting at 60s (not 10s — POC #8 showed 429s need 60-180s waits on S0 tier). Stagger parallel pass launches by 2-3s to avoid simultaneous token bursts against the shared TPM quota.
- Classification + metadata: retry 2x, 60s backoff. If fail → default to type: unknown, title: filename, pastor: unknown
- Segment classification: retry 1x. If fail → store transcript without segment labels, frontend shows unsegmented text
- Overall orchestrator timeout: 10 minutes
- On any permanent failure: update Cosmos DB status to failed with error message and failed_at timestamp

Note: S0 Standard tier rate limits (50-80K TPM) are tight for longer sermons. An 8K-word transcript (normal for 45-50 min sermons) with 3-4 parallel passes easily hits the ceiling. POC #8 confirmed this is not an edge case. If rate limits become a persistent problem, options: (1) request quota increase, (2) stagger passes sequentially instead of parallel (adds ~30s latency), (3) upgrade to higher tier.

## WPM Sanity Check

After transcription, check WPM. If < 80 or > 200, flag as suspect but continue processing. Store wpm_flag: true in results so frontend can show a warning.

## Upload Retry

No resume mechanism for failed uploads in MVP. If upload fails mid-transfer (network drop), user must retry from scratch. Frontend shows clear error with retry option. Phase 1: consider chunked upload or direct-to-blob via SAS token.

## Depends On

Infra (sermon-1a3) being deployed. POC #7 validates the full pipeline produces accurate scores on complete transcripts. POC #8 validates pipeline works across different preachers and sermon lengths (8K words / 48 min).

## Cost Target

~$0.75 per sermon ($0.67 speech + $0.09 OpenAI). POC #7 confirmed $0.09 OpenAI on 3,762-word transcript. POC #8 confirmed $0.76 on 8,192-word transcript.

title: POC #9: Spurgeon historical benchmark (text-only scoring)
close_reason: Scored 3 Spurgeon sermons: Compel Them to Come In (85.9), Immutability of God (72.8), Power of the Holy Spirit (80.7). Text-only scoring validated. Zero 429 errors with throttle-aware scheduling. Results in poc/spurgeon_benchmark_results.json, documented in docs/research.md.

title: Podcast RSS subscription — auto-score new episodes
description: Allow users to subscribe to a podcast RSS feed URL. System polls for new episodes, downloads audio, and automatically runs the PSR pipeline. Enables passive monitoring of pastors/churches without manual uploads. Discovery: DBC Morning Messages feed (dentonbible.org/mediafiles/denton-bible-morning-messages.xml) has 274+ episodes with direct MP3 enclosure URLs — standard podcast RSS makes this straightforward.

title: POC #7: Validate multipass scoring on full transcript
description: Re-run POC #5 multipass scoring on POC #6's complete transcript (3,762 words vs 1,064). Validates that scores are accurate on full text. Result: composite 88.0 vs 82.7 — partial transcript underestimated by 5.3 points. Application +13, Clarity +10, Delivery +10. Scripture refs: 12 vs 6. See poc/validated_multipass_poc.py and docs/research.md POC #7 section.
close_reason: Completed. Composite PSR 88.0 on full transcript (vs 82.7 on partial). All findings documented in docs/research.md and poc/README.md.

title: Build MVP Frontend
description:
Full spec at docs/frontend-spec.md. Three pages:

1. Upload page (/) — drag-and-drop audio file, redirect to detail page
2. Sermon feed (/sermons) — ESPN QBR-style sortable table
3. Sermon detail (/sermons/{id}) — Lighthouse-style scorecard with radar chart

Tech: Next.js + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS + Recharts
Deploy: Azure Static Web Apps (free tier)

Key Design Decisions:
- Lighthouse report pattern for the detail page (big score, category grid, expandable reasoning)
- Opta-style 8-axis radar chart as secondary visual
- Three score colors only: green (70+), yellow (50-69), red (below 50)
- Light theme only for MVP
- No component library — Tailwind utilities only
- Poll for processing status (no websockets)

API Contracts:
- POST /api/sermons (upload)
- GET /api/sermons (feed list)
- GET /api/sermons/{id} (full detail)

See docs/frontend-spec.md for wireframes, data contracts, component inventory, and design tokens.
UI inspiration research in bead sermon-3bz.

title: UI Design Research and Inspiration
description:
## UI Design Research — Inspiration Sources and Patterns

### Status
Research complete. Frontend spec written at docs/frontend-spec.md — supersedes the layout proposals below.

### Key Decision Update
Upload page includes optional title and pastor name fields (not in original research). LLM extraction isn't always reliable, so user-provided values take priority when given.

### Design Philosophy
Minimalist. No AI slop. Let the data speak. Every element earns its place.

---

## Sources Researched
1. ESPN QBR Leaderboard — sortable table pattern for sermon feed
2. Opta Player Radars — 8-axis radar chart for sermon detail
3. Rotten Tomatoes Score Box — composite score hero + summary pattern
4. Google Lighthouse — category gauge grid with expandable reasoning
5. Chronicle.church — sermon card design, text-forward list layout
6. Minimal Podcast App — anti-noise philosophy, typography-first
7. QB Throwing Scorecards — screenshot-able shareable card (Phase 1)
8. Credit Score Displays — circular gauge with factors list

All patterns incorporated into docs/frontend-spec.md.

title: Throttle-aware rate limit scheduler for LLM passes
close_reason: Implemented throttle-aware scheduler in azure_multipass_poc.py and validated_multipass_poc.py. Estimates tokens per pass, groups by deployment, runs parallel only when safe. Updated research.md and sermonplan.md with learnings.
